passages.title: How can you manage money well?
paragraphs: The winner is my good friend, Chen Jie. She's a 12-year-old girl from Class One, Grade Six.

We all know it's difficult to manage our money well, but Chen Jie has her special ways. She writes a spending plan. Every week she gets her pocket money. She also gets some money for selling waste and old things. She keeps her money in three money jars. Jar 1 is spending money. She uses half this money to buy goods like books. She also uses it to pay for services like haircuts. Jar 2 is money to share. She uses this to buy gifts, for example, birthday gifts for her family. Jar 3 is money to save. Chen Jie puts money there to save up for big things like a new bike. She really manages her money well!

passages.title: A long history of money
paragraphs: A long time ago, people exchanged goods or services. Sometimes it was difficult to get the things they wanted.
Later, people used different forms of money. But what did the money look like? Look! There is shell money, knife money, copper coins, ...
But metal coins were heavy to carry. So people used paper money.
We still use paper money today. We also have bankbooks and cards.
